title: eumachia obovoidea subsp. villosistipula
intro: Eumachia obovoidea subsp. villosistipula, also known as the Villose-stipuled Eumachia, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Eumachia obovoidea subsp. villosistipula can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
